rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND ALLEN et al VS. ALLIED PACKING et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, SEPTEMBER 14, 2007, LINE 5, DEFENDANT PHILLIPS ELECTRONICS NORTH AMERICA CORPORATION'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT HAS NOT NEGATED THE DETAILED FACTS OF PLAINTIFF'S EXPOSURE TO DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS AS DESCRIBED IN PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO ESTABLISH THAT IT DID NOT PURCHASE THE ASSETS OF PHILCO. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: FRED C. ARKO JR. VS. HANSON, BRIDGETT, MARCUS, VLAHOS & RUDY, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Hanson, Bridgett, Marcus, Vlahos & Rudy, Llp, Richard Stratton, James Holden, Walter Schneider'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Transferred From Dept. 301, Judge Busch Has Recused Himself From This Case.)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, SEPTEMBER 14, 2007, LINE 13, Defendants Hanson, Bridgett, Marcus, Vlahos & Rudy, LLP, Richard Stratton, James Holden, and Walter Schneider's Motion For Summary Judgment (Transferred From Dept. 301, Judge Busch Has Recused Himself From This Case). THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS CITE NO CALIFORNIA AUTHORITY ON POINT FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT AS A MATTER OF PUBLIC POLICY, THE MOTION MUST BE GRANTED. DEFENDANTS CONTENTIONS THAT SUMMARY JUDGMENT SHOULD BE GRANTED, BASED UPON IN PARI DELECTO AND UNCLEAN HANDS, ARE REALLY JUST THE PUBLIC POLICY ARGUMENT RESTATED. MOREOVER, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO THE VALUE, IF ANY, OF THE FREEMAN STOCK, AND IF IT HAD VALUE, WHETHER THE USE OF SUCH EVIDENCE WAS A VIABLE TRIAL STRATEGY. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE UNDERLYING JUDGMENT HAS BEEN SATISFIED. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED BECAUSE IT IS NOT CLEAR THAT THE BANKRUPTCY COURT DECIDED THE IDENTICAL ISSUE. =(302/PJM/LB)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT SANDERS VS. NANCY HEATH et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, SEPTEMBER 14, 2007, LINE 8, DEFENDANT NANCY HEATH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORY. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PARAGRAPHS 4 AND 5 OF PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE OF A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM/PB)
